frames: The agent stands upright, then begins to lean its torso backward while lifting its right leg slightly.
The agent continues to lean backward, bending its left knee and raising its right leg, with arms slightly bent.
The agent's torso is heavily bent backward, with both knees bent and arms raised, appearing unstable.
The agent maintains a backward lean, then starts to shift its weight forward, extending its right leg.
The agent rapidly shifts its torso forward, bringing its arms in, as if preparing to run.
The agent is now in a low, forward-leaning running posture, with its right leg bent and left leg extended back.
The agent propels forward, with its left leg off the ground and right leg pushing, initiating a run.
The agent continues its running gait, smoothly swinging its left leg forward while the right leg pushes off.
